checks.details: Commit `ec30ba8` changes exactly match scope: restores compact PLP H1 via higher-specificity CSS for shop/category/tag archives and stabilizes PLP filter parity E2E by resolving an existing category URL when slug env is missing.
Commit `47124d8` adds verifier MODE=practical/technical strict-JSON outputs, updates critic PHASE=2 requirements to require both verifier outputs, and adds `tasks/pipeline-json` scaffold.
UI/Woo-relevant changes: L1 (woo flow) is PASS (3 tests). L0 suite is PASS (with allowed SKIPs). a11y is PASS (forced=true per report). L2 is SKIP (optional unless policy requires).

evidence: Artifacts: artifacts/verify/verify-all.log includes `=== VERIFY ALL: OK ===`.
Artifacts: artifacts/verify/verify-report.json shows `exit_code: 0`, base_url = staging, PASS for l1 and a11y, and PASS for l0 with SKIPs tracked.
Diff: commit `ec30ba8` modifies e2e/plp-filter-url-parity.spec.js and adds PLP H1 override in theme CSS.
Diff: commit `47124d8` updates .cursor agent docs and adds tasks/pipeline-json scaffold.
risks: `php` not found caused `l0:php-lint` SKIP (allowed). This can hide PHP syntax errors if not run in CI/another environment.
`resolveCategoryUrl()` uses a hardcoded staging origin only for parsing absolute hrefs; pathname is returned.
required_next_steps: If you want stricter L0 coverage: ensure PHP is available in PATH (so `php -l` runs) or run `verify:l0` in CI where PHP is installed.
If release policy requires them: run with `VERIFY_LINKCHECK=1` and/or `VERIFY_LIGHTHOUSE=1` and attach resulting artifacts.
